verses.commentary: All this I have told you so that you will not fall away. Skandalizō (σκανδαλίζω), stumble, be offended. Warning purpose. Forewarning prevents falling. Persecution preparation.

They will put you out of the synagogue; in fact, the time is coming when anyone who kills you will think they are offering a service to God. Excommunication and execution. Aposynagōgos (ἀποσυνάγωγος), expelled. Murderers will claim worship. Religious killing. Sincere but deadly zeal.
They will do such things because they have not known the Father or Me. Root cause. Ignorance of God. Not knowing Father or Jesus produces violence. Paul exemplifies (1 Timothy 1:13).

I have told you this, so that when their time comes you will remember that I warned you about them. I did not tell you this from the beginning because I was with you. Remembrance goal. Their time (hōra, ὥρα): persecution hour. Earlier silence explained. His presence protected. Now forewarning necessary.

But now I am going to Him who sent Me. None of you asks Me, Where are You going? Departure announced. Strange observation. They asked in 13:36, 14:5. Perhaps they stopped asking. Grief silences questions.
Rather, you are filled with grief because I have said these things. Lupē (λύπη), sorrow. Grief over departure. Persecution announcement compounds sadness. Present pain obscures future good.

But very truly I tell you, it is for your good that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send Him to you. Paradox. Departure is advantage. Sympherei (συμφέρει), profitable, beneficial. Condition: Jesus must leave for Spirit to come. Departure enables sending. Trade: physical Jesus for universal Spirit.

When He comes, He will prove the world to be in the wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment. Spirit's prosecutorial work. Elenchō (ἐλέγχω), convict, expose. Threefold conviction. World's error exposed.
About sin, because people do not believe in Me. Sin defined. Unbelief is the sin. Rejection of Jesus. Root sin from which others flow.

About righteousness, because I am going to the Father, where you can see Me no longer. Righteousness proven. Ascension validates. Father receives Jesus. Vindication. Jesus' righteousness confirmed by exaltation.

And about judgment, because the prince of this world now stands condemned. Judgment executed. Satan judged. Kekritai (κέκριται), perfect tense, already judged. Cross defeats Satan. Condemnation stands.

I have much more to say to you, more than you can now bear. Pedagogical restraint. Current incapacity. Bastazō (βαστάζω), bear, carry. Too much truth now would overwhelm. Progressive revelation necessary.

But when He, the Spirit of truth, comes, He will guide you into all the truth. He will not speak on His own; He will speak only what He hears, and He will tell you what is yet to come. Spirit's teaching ministry. Hodēgēsei (ὁδηγήσει), guide, lead. Into all truth. Not independent but subordinate. Hears and speaks. Future revelation. Eschatological insight.

He will glorify Me because it is from Me that He will receive what He will make known to you. Spirit glorifies Jesus. Christocentric focus. Receives from Jesus. Transmits to disciples. Spirit's ministry points to Son.

All that belongs to the Father is Mine. That is why I said the Spirit will receive from Me what He will make known to you. Trinitarian unity. Father's possessions are Jesus'. Spirit receives from Jesus who receives from Father. Chain of revelation.
Jesus went on to say, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me. Mikron (μικρόν), a little. Double enigma. Brief absence. Brief reunion. Cryptic timing.

At this, some of His disciples said to one another, What does He mean by saying, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me, and Because I am going to the Father? Confusion. They repeat His words to each other. Meaning eludes them.
They kept asking, What does He mean by a little while? We don't understand what He is saying. Persistent bewilderment. Little while unclear. Total incomprehension admitted.

Jesus saw that they wanted to ask Him about this, so He said to them, Are you asking one another what I meant when I said, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me? Jesus perceives their confusion. Addresses unasked question. Divine knowledge of thoughts.

Very truly I tell you, you will weep and mourn while the world rejoices. You will grieve, but your grief will turn to joy. Contrasting responses. Disciples: weeping, mourning. World: rejoicing. Crucifixion day. But reversal coming. Grief temporary. Joy permanent. Resurrection transforms.

A woman giving birth to a child has pain because her time has come; but when her baby is born she forgets the anguish because of her joy that a child is born into the world. Birth analogy. Labor pain intense but temporary. Joy eclipses pain. Memory of suffering fades. New life brings joy.

So with you: Now is your time of grief, but I will see you again and you will rejoice, and no one will take away your joy. Application. Present grief. Future joy. Resurrection reunion. Permanent joy. Outhen (οὐδεὶς), no one can remove. Secure gladness.

In that day you will no longer ask Me anything. Very truly I tell you, My Father will give you whatever you ask in My name. That day: resurrection and beyond. Questions cease. Full understanding. Prayer promise. Direct access to Father through Jesus' name.

Until now you have not asked for anything in My name. Ask and you will receive, and your joy will be complete. Past failure to pray in His name. New invitation. Ask. Receive. Joy completed. Peplērōmenē (πεπληρωμένη), filled full.

Though I have been speaking figuratively, a time is coming when I will no longer use this kind of language but will tell you plainly about My Father. Paroimiais (παροιμίαις), figures, proverbs. Current veiled speech. Future plain speech. After resurrection, clarity.
In that day you will ask in My name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf. Direct prayer. Jesus' name gives access. Not that He won't intercede, but they have immediate access.

No, the Father Himself loves you because you have loved Me and have believed that I came from God. Father's love for disciples. Philei (φιλεῖ), affectionate love. Cause: their love for Jesus and belief in His origin.
I came from the Father and entered the world; now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father. Summary. From Father. Into world. Out of world. Back to Father. Complete incarnation cycle.
Then Jesus' disciples said, Now You are speaking clearly and without figures of speech. Relief. Plain speech appreciated. They think they understand now.

Now we can see that You know all things and that You do not even need to have anyone ask You questions. This makes us believe that You came from God. Confession of omniscience. Knowledge without inquiry. Prophetic insight. Belief in divine origin.
Do you now believe? Jesus' question. Arti (ἄρτι), now. Gentle challenge. Their confidence will soon fail.

A time is coming and in fact has come when you will be scattered, each to your own home. You will leave Me all alone. Yet I am not alone, for My Father is with Me. Prophecy of abandonment. Skorpisthēsesthe (σκορπισθήσεσθε), scattered. Zechariah 13:7. Each to own home. Jesus left alone. But not truly alone. Father's presence abides.

I have told you these things, so that in Me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world. Purpose: peace in Jesus. In world: thlipsis (θλῖψις), tribulation, pressure. Guaranteed trouble. But: tharseo (θαρσέω), take courage. Perfect tense: nenikēka (νενίκηκα), I have conquered. Victory already won. World defeated.
